factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit real yield data in news window. US Treasury yields remain mixed as markets await Fed minutes (Wednesday release). Without fresh TIPS data or clear yield directional move, this remains neutral to slightly soft.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: Fed Governor Christopher Waller reinforced 2% inflation commitment and appears hawkish on rates staying elevated. Bloomberg reports Waller and Warsh diverging on forward guidance, with Warsh stripping guidance language from statements—suggesting potential hawkish tilt. Markets pricing elevated rates for longer, which is headwind for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Bloomberg headline: 'Traders Most Positive on Dollar Since 2015 as Fed Hike Looms.' DXY near 100.90, supported by elevated rate expectations. Strong dollar is inversely correlated with gold and is headwind. GBP/JPY at 18-year highs (217+) signals broad USD strength momentum.

factors.name: US Nominal Yields Direction
factors.summary: No fresh Treasury 10y yield move reported in past 24h. Gold headline from Bloomberg states 'Gold Steadies as Traders Look to Fed for Interest-Rate Outlook'—implies waiting mode, not directional pressure. Yields mixed, awaiting Wednesday Fed minutes.

factors.name: Inflation Prints & Expectations
factors.summary: No recent CPI, PCE, or PPI data in news window. Japan wage data shows cash earnings +3.2% y/y (vs +3.4% expected)—slightly soft but not a major US inflation signal. Inflation expectations appear stable; no surprise prints to drive gold.

factors.name: Japan (BoJ, Yen Carry, USD/JPY)
factors.summary: Hedge funds most negative on JPY since 2007 as USD/JPY grinds higher throughout 2026. BoJ signals remain loose/accommodative, supporting USD/JPY strength. Weak yen and strong dollar both headwind for gold; carry unwind risk remains limited.

factors.name: Oil & Energy (Geopolitical Proxy)
factors.summary: Oil holds drop at $68.55; Strait of Hormuz ship attack reported (tanker hit by projectile, fire reported) but no major escalation. Saudi Arabia cutting prices signals oversupply concerns. Oil weakness typically reduces inflation expectations, neutral to bearish for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tensions
factors.summary: Hormuz ship incident reported but appears contained (no casualties/environmental damage). Russia-Ukraine drone strikes continue but no material escalation. Middle East tension present but priced in; no fresh shock driving safe-haven bid into gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equities
factors.summary: US stocks closed strongly: Dow record 53,061, Nasdaq +1.12% led by chip strength (Samsung profit surge, SK Hynix ADR listing, Broadcom/Apple deal extended). Strong equity rally reduces near-term safe-haven demand for gold; risk-on environment headwind.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Demand
factors.summary: No news on GLD/IAU flows or PBoC/RBI buying in this window. Structural CB demand remains bid (longer-term) but no fresh catalyst in 24h. Silver weakness (-1% on Monday, threatening $60) suggests mild de-risking in precious metals complex.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens
factors.summary: USD strength (DXY near 100.90, most positive sentiment since 2015) is pulling bid away from gold. Bitcoin mentioned in context of Trump's strategic reserve plan, with turf war between agencies, but no major 'digital gold' narrative shift. Swiss franc not mentioned.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts (24-72h)
factors.summary: Fed minutes release Wednesday (high impact for real yields and forward guidance interpretation). RBNZ decision this week (NZ dollar sensitive, indirect). No major US data (NFP, CPI, PCE) imminent in next 48h. Japan wage/spending data already released.

screening.narrative: Gold is stuck in stasis, up fractionally on the day (−0.10% in 4h, −0.00% in 24h) as the market awaits Fed minutes on Wednesday for clues on rate trajectory. The near-term narrative is dominated by hawkish Fed positioning (Waller's inflation commitment reinforced, Warsh pulling forward guidance) and extraordinarily strong USD sentiment (most positive since 2015), both headwinds for gold. Equity strength (record Dow, Nasdaq +1.12% on chip rally) reduces immediate safe-haven demand. Oil and geopolitics offer no fresh shock catalysts. Real yields appear stable but elevated; nominal yields anchored by forward-rate expectations rather than fresh data.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes (Wednesday, Jul 9 ~2pm ET) – key for real yield and forward guidance interpretation
RBNZ Decision (this week, likely 25bp hike per markets) – indirect impact via USD/NZD and rate differentials
No major US inflation data (CPI, PCE) until mid-month; NFP not due until early August
Potential Hormuz escalation (ship attack mentioned, ongoing geopolitical risk but contained so far)
screening.risk_warning: A dovish repricing from Fed minutes (if Powell/Waller signal pause or rate-cuts sooner) could spike gold by 1-2% in hours. Conversely, a fresh inflation surprise (Michigan sentiment, oil spike on Hormuz escalation) could hammer gold lower. Equity correction would provide safe-haven bid. Strong dollar momentum appears entrenched; USD weakness would be the single biggest gold bullish catalyst but has not materialized despite soft jobs data last week.
screening.entry_guidance: No clear directional entry warranted. Gold is in holding pattern ahead of Wednesday Fed minutes; avoid chasing on current 4h/24h flatness. Consider nibbling long on pullback to $4,160 support IF Fed minutes disappoint (dovish surprise), or short on break above $4,190 if minutes are hawkish. Best tactical entry post-Wednesday catalyst resolution.
